title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Mr. Queene explains to Colonel Melrose and Mr. Satterthwaite that the perceived truth regarding the crime was a staged performance inspired by a book. Mr. Queer and Mr. Quincannon clarify that Dulwich climbed through a window to knock out James and assist a lady doctor in staging the scene. They discuss how the culprits manipulated a clock and a watch to create a false narrative, noting that the valet would have known how to manipulate the watch hands to match the clock. Mr. Queene reveals his intention to save Jennings from the gallows, aided by a piece of evidence held by Mr. Satterthwaite: a fragment of curved glass. This glass proves the watch was not broken in James's pocket, but was instead removed, opened, and tampered with before being returned.

In a separate narrative, Joyce Lambert visits an employment agency in England to seek day work, but she is rejected due to a lack of credentials. Despite her hunger and panic, she maintains her composure and meets her Aunt Mary at a bar near Victoria Street. Aunt Mary, concerned by Joyce's thin appearance, informs her that she cannot provide lodging because she has rented her house to short-term tenants and is traveling to Harrogate for rheumatism. Joyce subsequently walks through various London streets to a dingy house on Euston Road. There, she enters an attic and is greeted by Tris, an old, coarse-haired fox terrier with cloudy eyes.
